note: seq=49 (ATLAS->Lead-Hunter HANDOFF EXPORTER -- this is what makes prospects/hour climb). READ-ONLY exporter: selects atlas.business rows that HAVE a crawlable website/domain, ranks them real-time-first (Tier1 CT/SSL+MX -> Tier4 Overture), drops .gov/.mil, repairs mojibake, de-dupes via a box cursor, and PUBLISHES them as OSM-shaped JSONL to status/hetzner/atlas-feed/atlas-feed-<batch>.jsonl + atlas-feed-latest.json (via the GitHub Contents API + STATUS_TOKEN already in /etc/atlas/autopull.env -- same status-back as the metrics exporter; NO new port, NO PG exposure, NO DB creds on WordPress). The WP-side tnc-atlas-feeder GETs those static files every 5 min and feeds the hunter's existing qualify->promote pipeline (atlas_business_id becomes the 4th dedupe key). HARD READ-ONLY: every query runs in SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY and is rolled back -- no DML/DDL possible. Schema INTROSPECTED (name/website|domain/phone/city/state/category/lat/lon resolved from information_schema; if website resolves to nothing the exporter refuses to run and says so). FAIL-SOFT: DB down/missing -> honest reachable:false manifest + exit 0 (timer never wedges); GitHub unreachable -> JSONL+manifest written locally, exit 0. Installs a 10-min oneshot timer + kicks one run NON-BLOCKING now (systemctl start --no-block, so a full export can never wedge the 90s puller). HONEST: not run against live PG from the sandbox; py_compile clean, sha pin matches byte-for-byte (003daa1d...), and a mock-cursor harness passed tier-ranking + .gov/.mil drop + dedupe + mojibake + valid-JSON-per-line. Tiers 1-2 only fill once CT/MX provenance exists; until then rows land Tier 3-4 (high-fit/breadth) which is correct, not a bug. Allowlist-safe (every run_allowlisted starts with /opt/atlas/); timer keeps Unit=atlas-autopull.service untouched.

steps.note: seq=49: read-only ATLAS->hunter handoff exporter; publishes status/hetzner/atlas-feed/*; makes prospects/hour climb.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/importers/atlas_handoff_export.py':'003daa1d1501d7b7852bd789a6c839b84ca6652ac8001fa4f2b2570ba9d24249'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS -> Lead Hunter handoff exporter (read-only; publishes status/hetzner/atlas-feed/*.jsonl for the WP tnc-atlas-feeder)
After=network-online.target postgresql.service
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
Nice=12
IOSchedulingClass=idle
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/db.env
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/autopull.env
ExecStart=/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python /opt/atlas/importers/atlas_handoff_export.py
TimeoutStartSec=10min

[Unit]
Description=ATLAS handoff exporter -- feed new ATLAS businesses to the Lead Hunter every 10 min

[Timer]
OnBootSec=4min
OnUnitActiveSec=10min
Persistent=true
Unit=atlas-handoff-export.service

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import subprocess as s; s.run(['systemctl','reset-failed','atlas-handoff-export.service']); s.run(['systemctl','start','--no-block','atlas-handoff-export.service']); print('handoff export kicked (non-blocking)')"
